title: cordula boxallii
intro: Cordula boxallii, also known as Mountain Mint or Boxwood, is a perennial plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe and Asia, characterized by its small, white flowers and distinctive minty fragrance. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues such as indigestion and nausea, as well as its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Cordula boxallii include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an expectorant to ease congestion. Herbal preparations of Cordula boxallii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to make teas, salves, and ointments for topical application.
